title: Usha Sahay: Understanding Media for More Impact on Policy
summary: During the Obama presidency, then-undergraduate Usha Sahay began reading about the role of the U.S. in two conflicts that dominated the news: the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan. Her interest was sparked in both journalism and international affairs; today she is a Harvard Kennedy School student and Belfer International and Global Affairs Student Fellow interested in U.S. leadership and decision-making in foreign affairs and the role of the media in how policy is crafted.
body: Usha Sahay is a Belfer International and Global Affairs Student Fellow The latest from Belfer See more During the Obama presidency, then-undergraduate Usha Sahay began reading about the role of the U.S. in two conflicts that dominated the news: the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan. Her interest was sparked in both journalism and international affairs; today she is a Harvard Kennedy School student and Belfer International and Global Affairs Student Fellow interested in U.S. leadership and decision-making in foreign affairs and the role of the media in how policy is crafted. Sahay, who has worked for The Wall Street Journal and HuffPost , began her media career at War on the Rocks , a digital national security and foreign policy publication where she was Managing Editor before coming to Harvard and remains Editor-at-Large. She sees the platform as a way to connect policymakers, scholars, and the media in discourse about foreign policy and international relations. “I kind of always had in the back of my head that I wanted to take those [journalism] skills and go back to the world of national security,” Sahay said. She remains invested in the role of the media in shaping the national security debate. “I realized the importance of being someone who really understood the media environment and the journalistic process in a granular way but also had a deep understanding of policy issues,” Sahay said. “One of the reasons I was excited to come to the Kennedy School was that there are a lot of fantastic resources on the journalism side here and it is a great place to deepen your understanding of national security issues.” Sahay is currently working on a podcast that will examine the history of nuclear weapons, with the goal of exploring unanswered puzzles and recurring historical themes. In addition, she is assisting Harvard Kennedy School Professor and historian Fredrik Logevall with research for his upcoming two-volume biography of JFK. Working with Prof. Logevall is “a really great opportunity to learn from someone at the top of their field and get an understanding of the nitty gritty of how a book gets researched and edited,” Sahay said. Recommended citation Grebbin, Shelby. "Usha Sahay: Understanding Media for More Impact on Policy." Belfer Center Newsletter, Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs, Harvard Kennedy School. (Fall/Winter 2019-2020). result.main_content_en: The article profiles Usha Sahay, a Harvard Kennedy School student and Belfer International and Global Affairs Student Fellow. Her career interests were sparked by the U.S. wars in Iraq and Afghanistan, leading to a dual focus on journalism and international affairs. She has worked for media outlets like The Wall Street Journal, HuffPost, and War on the Rocks, where she served as Managing Editor. Sahay views platforms like War on the Rocks as vital for connecting policymakers, scholars, and the media to discuss foreign policy. She emphasizes the importance of deeply understanding both the media environment and substantive policy issues to effectively shape the national security debate. Currently, she is working on a podcast about the history of nuclear weapons and assisting Professor Fredrik Logevall with research for a biography of JFK.
result.main_content_zh: 本文介绍了哈佛肯尼迪学院学生、贝尔弗国际与全球事务学生研究员乌莎·萨海。她对新闻和国际事务的兴趣源于美国在伊拉克和阿富汗的战争。她的职业生涯横跨新闻与政策，曾任职于《华尔街日报》、赫芬顿邮报以及数字国家安全与外交政策出版物“War on the Rocks”，并在后者担任过执行主编。她将该平台视为连接政策制定者、学者和媒体进行外交政策讨论的重要桥梁。萨海强调，必须深入理解媒体环境、新闻流程以及具体的政策问题，才能有效影响国家安全议题的讨论。目前，她正在制作一个探讨核武器历史的播客，并协助哈佛肯尼迪学院的弗雷德里克·洛格瓦尔教授为其即将出版的肯尼迪传记进行研究。
